MCP becoming table stakes for all frameworks by mid-2026

Evaluation based on complexity, team expertise, integration needs
Ranked by shipping experience in production

stats.fact: Agentic AI market projected to reach $10.86 billion in 2026, up from $7.55 billion in 2025
Market expected to reach $196.6 billion by 2034 at 43.8% CAGR
Gartner projects 40% of enterprise applications will include task-specific AI agents by end of 2026
51% of companies have already deployed AI agents, but only 1 in 9 runs them in production
96% of enterprises are expanding AI agent use; 83% of executives view agentic AI investment as essential
LangGraph has 34.5 million monthly downloads; used by Uber, Klarna, LinkedIn, JPMorgan, 400+ companies
CrewAI at 45,900+ GitHub stars, powers 12 million daily agent executions, 100,000+ certified developers
Klarna's AI assistant (LangGraph) handles support for 85 million users, reducing resolution time by 80%
Open-source frameworks have 55% lower cost-per-agent than platform solutions, but 2.3x more setup time
Agent framework setup costs $50-100K vs $500K-1M for traditional workflows; saves up to $250K annually replacing 10 operators

frameworks.name: LangGraph
frameworks.best_for: Complex production workflows with branching logic, audit trails, enterprise
cons: Steepest learning curve (~120 lines for ReAct)
frameworks.pricing: MIT-licensed free; LangSmith free tier 5K traces/month, Plus $39/seat/month, Enterprise custom

frameworks.name: CrewAI
frameworks.best_for: Fast prototyping, team-based workflows, business users
frameworks.pricing: Free tier 50 executions; $25/month 100 executions; Enterprise custom up to 30K executions with SOC2/SSO

frameworks.name: AutoGen / AG2
frameworks.best_for: Code generation, research tasks, iterative critique workflows

frameworks.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
frameworks.best_for: Teams already on OpenAI, clean agent handoffs
frameworks.pricing: Free SDK; pay for OpenAI API usage

frameworks.name: Google ADK
frameworks.pricing: Free SDK; pay for Gemini API / Google Cloud

frameworks.name: Smolagents (Hugging Face)
frameworks.best_for: Local LLMs, minimal code, rapid experimentation

angles: No single best framework: LangGraph for production control, CrewAI for speed, AutoGen for conversation — match architecture to use case
The adoption-to-production gap: 51% deployed agents but only 1 in 9 in production — frameworks that close this gap win
MCP is the new table stakes: by mid-2026, frameworks without native MCP feel incomplete
Open-source vs platform trade-off: 55% lower cost but 2.3x more setup time
Enterprise convergence: LangGraph dominating enterprise with audit trails, checkpointing, rollback
The local LLM angle: 32B+ models now viable for multi-agent pipelines on consumer hardware
Cost reality: $50-100K for agent framework vs $500K-1M for traditional automation
